summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by expressing his love and pride for the Impact Church family. He then introduced the sermon 'Addition by Subtraction,' exploring the biblical principle that spiritual growth and fulfillment come not from adding more to our lives, but from subtracting the things that hinder our walk with Christ. Drawing from the Apostle Paul's writings in Philippians, Pastor Travis emphasized that true life is found in Christ alone, and to die is gain because it means being with Him. The core message was that we become more like Jesus—more loving, secure, and compassionate—as we decrease and He increases in our lives. The sermon focused on two primary areas requiring detoxification: our thought life and our friend circle. Pastor Travis taught that our minds, like a spiritual stomach, are shaped by what we feed them, and we must guard our hearts as temples of the Holy Spirit. He also stressed the importance of being intentional about our closest friendships, choosing to run the spiritual marathon with those who are on the 'same race, same pace' toward Christ. The call was to make hard, prayerful decisions to remove mental and relational toxins, trusting that less of the world's clutter means more of God's clarity, power, and joy.
mainPoints: Spiritual maturity is achieved through addition by subtraction: we grow in Christ by decreasing our own desires and removing toxic influences from our lives.
We must detoxify our thought life by guarding what we feed our minds, recognizing our bodies as temples of the Holy Spirit.
We must detoxify our friend circle by intentionally choosing close friends who are running the 'same race, same pace' toward Christ, as they define our future.

versesMentioned.text: For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.

But whatever were gains to me I now consider loss for the sake of Christ. What is more, I consider everything a loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whose sake I have lost all things. I consider them garbage, that I may gain Christ
He must become greater; I must become less.
When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.

Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith.
For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.

Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.
As water reflects the face, so one’s life reflects the heart.

You were running a good race. Who cut in on you to keep you from obeying the truth? That kind of persuasion does not come from the one who calls you. 'A little yeast works through the whole batch of dough.'
Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?
Do not be misled: 'Bad company corrupts good character.'

But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days. People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy, without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good, treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God— having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people.

Monday.scripture: Philippians 1:21 (NIV) - 'For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.'
Monday.action: Write down this verse and place it somewhere you will see it often. Let it reframe your perspective on what truly matters today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to live today with the perspective that my life is for Christ. May my choices and thoughts reflect that He is my ultimate gain.

Tuesday.scripture: John 3:30 (NIV) - 'He must become greater; I must become less.'
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where your pride, insecurity, or desire for control is prominent. Consciously choose to step back in that area today.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I invite you to increase in my life. Help me to decrease, surrendering my will and ego to you so that your character shines through me.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - 'Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.'
Wednesday.action: Audit one source of media you consume (social media, news, entertainment). Unfollow, mute, or take a break from any account or channel that brings anxiety or pollutes your thoughts.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, show me what is polluting my mind. Give me the courage to guard my heart by removing toxic influences and filling that space with your truth.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:19-20 (NIV) - '...your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit... Therefore honor God with your bodies.'
Thursday.action: Do one thing today that honors God with your body. This could be choosing healthy food, resting, speaking kindly, or avoiding something you know is harmful.
Thursday.prayer: God, thank you that your Spirit lives in me. Help me to remember that my body is your temple, and guide my choices to honor you in how I treat it.

Friday.scripture: Proverbs 27:19 (NIV) - 'As water reflects the face, so one’s life reflects the heart.'
Friday.action: Reflect on your three closest friends. Do they push you toward Christ? Prayerfully consider if any relationship needs to be moved from 'close' to 'casual' for your spiritual health.
Friday.prayer: Father, give me wisdom about my friendships. Help me to see clearly who reflects a heart for you and gives me the courage to choose friends who help me run my race for you.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 6:14 (NIV) - 'Do not be yoked together with unbelievers...'
Saturday.action: If you are in a close relationship (dating, business, deep friendship) with someone not fully committed to Christ, have an honest, prayerful conversation about your spiritual priorities this week.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, protect me from being unequally yoked. Guide my relationships so that I am running 'the same race, same pace' with those who are fully devoted to you. Give me clarity and peace.
